title: hibbertia improna
intro: Hibbertia improna, also known as Guinea flower or Guinea gold, is a species of flowering plant in the family Dilleniaceae. It is native to Australia and has been traditionally used in Aboriginal medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are believed to provide health benefits such as wound healing, reduced inflammation, and improved immune function. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, tinctures, and salves, which are used to treat a range of health issues, including skin conditions and respiratory problems.
